authorship.artifact_policy: Cases are written from neutral behavioral requirements. No external conformance fixtures, scenario text, or test implementations are used.

cases.requirement: Integer addition returns the arithmetic sum.
cases.query: RETURN 1 + 2 AS value

cases.requirement: Multiplication binds more tightly than addition.
cases.query: RETURN 2 + 3 * 4 AS value

cases.requirement: A named parameter contributes its typed value to an expression.
cases.query: RETURN $left + $right AS value

cases.requirement: Coalesce returns the first non-null input.
cases.query: RETURN coalesce(null, null, 'ready') AS value

cases.requirement: IS NULL identifies a null value.
cases.query: RETURN null IS NULL AS value

cases.requirement: A generic CASE selects the first true branch.
cases.query: RETURN CASE WHEN 2 > 1 THEN 'yes' ELSE 'no' END AS value

cases.requirement: A simple CASE compares its operand with each branch value.
cases.query: RETURN CASE 2 WHEN 1 THEN 'one' WHEN 2 THEN 'two' ELSE 'other' END AS value

cases.requirement: AND, OR, XOR, and NOT follow three-valued logic: decisive operands yield booleans and undecidable combinations yield null.
cases.query: RETURN (true OR null) AS or_true, (true AND null) IS NULL AS and_unknown, (false AND null) AS and_false, (true XOR null) IS NULL AS xor_unknown, NOT false AS negated

cases.requirement: IN returns membership when decisive and null when a null list item prevents a decision.
cases.query: RETURN 2 IN [1, 2] AS present, 5 IN [1, 2] AS absent, (3 IN [1, null]) IS NULL AS unknown

cases.requirement: id() returns a non-null identity that is equal for the same node across bindings.
cases.query: MATCH (n:Item {id: 7}) MATCH (m:Item {id: 7}) RETURN id(n) = id(m) AS same, id(n) IS NOT NULL AS present

cases.requirement: A list literal preserves value order and types.
cases.query: RETURN [1, 'two', true, null] AS value

cases.requirement: A zero-based list index returns the selected item.
cases.query: RETURN [10, 20, 30][1] AS value

cases.requirement: An open-ended list slice returns the requested suffix.
cases.query: RETURN [10, 20, 30][1..] AS value

cases.requirement: List concatenation supports list/list and list/element forms.
cases.query: RETURN [1, 2] + [3] AS joined, [1, 2] + 3 AS appended

cases.requirement: any returns true when at least one item satisfies its predicate.
cases.query: RETURN any(x IN [1, 2, 3] WHERE x = 2) AS value

cases.requirement: all, none, and single return decisive boolean results for list predicates.
cases.query: RETURN all(x IN [1, 2] WHERE x > 0) AS every, none(x IN [1, 2] WHERE x > 5) AS nothing, single(x IN [1, 2] WHERE x = 1) AS exactly_one

cases.requirement: WHERE keeps only rows whose predicate is true; false and unknown rows are removed.
cases.query: MATCH (p:Person) WHERE p.age > 30 RETURN p.id AS id

cases.requirement: RETURN DISTINCT removes duplicate projected rows.
cases.query: UNWIND [1, 1, 2] AS x RETURN DISTINCT x AS value ORDER BY value

cases.requirement: WITH projects an aggregate into a fresh scope for later clauses.
cases.query: UNWIND [1, 2, 3] AS x WITH sum(x) AS total RETURN total

cases.requirement: SKIP offsets an ordered result and LIMIT caps its size.
cases.query: UNWIND [1, 2, 3, 4] AS x RETURN x AS value ORDER BY value SKIP 1 LIMIT 2

cases.requirement: UNWIND emits one row per list item and ORDER BY controls result order.
cases.query: UNWIND [3, 1, 2] AS value RETURN value ORDER BY value

cases.requirement: UNWIND of an empty list produces no rows.
cases.query: UNWIND [] AS x RETURN x

cases.requirement: UNWIND of null produces no rows.
cases.query: UNWIND null AS x RETURN x

cases.requirement: UNION removes duplicate rows across branches.
cases.query: RETURN 1 AS value UNION RETURN 1 AS value UNION RETURN 2 AS value

cases.requirement: UNION ALL preserves duplicate rows across branches.
cases.query: RETURN 1 AS value UNION ALL RETURN 1 AS value

cases.requirement: A CALL subquery imports an outer variable with WITH and runs once per input row.
cases.query: MATCH (p:Person) CALL { WITH p RETURN p.id * 2 AS doubled } RETURN doubled ORDER BY doubled

cases.requirement: A created node can be matched by label and property.
cases.query: MATCH (n:Person {id: 1}) RETURN n.name AS name

cases.requirement: A missing OPTIONAL MATCH extends the input row with null.
cases.query: MATCH (p:Person) OPTIONAL MATCH (p)-[:KNOWS]->(q) RETURN p.id AS id, q AS missing

cases.requirement: A created directed relationship retains its type and properties.
cases.query: MATCH (a:Person)-[r:KNOWS]->(b:Person) RETURN a.id AS source, b.id AS target, type(r) AS kind, r.since AS since

cases.requirement: An undirected relationship pattern can match an incoming stored relationship.
cases.query: MATCH (a:Person {id: 2})-[:KNOWS]-(b:Person) RETURN b.id AS id

cases.requirement: A bounded variable-length pattern matches every path length within its range.
cases.query: MATCH (a:Item {id: 1})-[:NEXT*1..2]->(n) RETURN n.id AS id ORDER BY id

cases.requirement: An unbounded variable-length pattern traverses at most ten hops: KGLite's runaway-query guard, an intentional divergence from unbounded traversal.
cases.query: MATCH (:Hop {id: 0})-[:NEXT*]->(n) RETURN count(n) AS reachable

cases.requirement: An open-ended variable-length minimum above the default ten-hop ceiling raises the ceiling to that minimum, so the range is never silently empty.
cases.query: MATCH (:Hop {id: 0})-[:NEXT*11..]->(n) RETURN n.id AS id

cases.requirement: SET assigns a property on a bound node.
cases.query: MATCH (n:Person) RETURN n.name AS name

cases.requirement: REMOVE deletes a property and subsequent access yields null.
cases.query: MATCH (n:Person) RETURN n.obsolete AS value

cases.requirement: Repeating the same node MERGE does not create a duplicate.
cases.query: MATCH (n:Person) RETURN count(n) AS count

cases.requirement: FOREACH applies its updating body once per list item.
cases.query: MATCH (n:Item) RETURN n.id AS id ORDER BY id

cases.requirement: DELETE removes a bound node with no relationships.
cases.query: MATCH (n:Item) RETURN count(n) AS count

cases.requirement: DETACH DELETE removes a bound node together with its relationships.
cases.query: MATCH (n:Item) RETURN count(n) AS count

cases.requirement: A one-edge path decomposes into two nodes and one relationship.
cases.query: MATCH path = (a:Item {id: 1})-[:NEXT]->(b:Item) RETURN size(nodes(path)) AS nodes, size(relationships(path)) AS relationships

cases.requirement: nodes(path) preserves traversal order from the start node to the end node.
cases.query: MATCH path = (a:Item {id: 1})-[:NEXT*2]->(c:Item) UNWIND nodes(path) AS n RETURN n.id AS id
